reasoning: The base rate for betting favorites winning the Nobel Peace Prize is historically between 10% and 20% once you remove the bookmakers' overround (vig). Sudan's Emergency Response Rooms (ERRs) were the favorite in 2025 and, while they did not win, they have remained a very strong contender for 2026. They are currently the betting favorite at around 3/1 to +300 (implied probability 25%). They also meet the stylistic criteria of the Norwegian Nobel Committee: they are a grassroots humanitarian organization operating in an under-reported, massive crisis. Furthermore, they are ranked #2 on the new PRIO Director's 2026 shortlist (by Nina Græger), and historically, being on this shortlist is a strong positive indicator. While they dropped from #1 (under former director Henrik Urdal) to #2, their thematic relevance is very high (the last pure humanitarian aid winner was the WFP in 2020). The Metaculus community prediction of 21% is well-calibrated with the true vig-adjusted odds. I anchor my initial probability at 21% and increase toward 0.5 for subsequent (distant) resolution milestones to reflect growing uncertainty over time.
